policy.reason: These commits are durable, focused-tested engineering evidence. They remain unpromoted and do not collectively satisfy complete persistence coverage, the required retail differentials, promoted-binary provenance, or the clean-install certification package.

slices.subject: Support persistent Fallout containers
scope: authored fixed contents
locked activation
key unlock
mutable contents save/load
limitations: No whole-corpus container differential
No promoted-binary provenance

anchor ownership

malformed schema rejection

slices.subject: Blend native Fallout cloud transitions
scope: all four FNV cloud layers preserve current and next authored texture endpoints
continuous weather transition factor replaces the abrupt 50-percent texture swap
current and next textures bind to distinct shader sampler units zero and one
real blend factor reaches the Fallout cloud shader
explicit transparent-black fallback clears absent transition endpoints without stale bindings
layer visibility follows the endpoint that contributes at the current factor
cloud speed and color continue to interpolate per layer
disabled cloud nodes apply the latest endpoints and blend when re-enabled
non-finite blend factors canonicalize to zero at weather interpolation, SkyManager ingress, and CloudUpdater ingress
proof logging exposes current texture, next texture, factor, speed, color, visibility, and resolved bindings
no shader source modification
limitations: Both endpoint samplers still share one interpolated per-layer TexMat and speed; independent current and next UV accumulation is unproved and unimplemented
No unobstructed natural exterior to interior to exterior visual differential has been accepted
Authored sparse alpha masks, clear directions, and horizon fade can still produce legitimately sparse visible clouds
No complete 98-WTHR cloud-asset resolution differential
Launcher environment and binary-provenance hardening is tracked in a separate worlds slice
Scenery LOD substitution and water reflection coverage are separate unresolved paths
No promoted-binary provenance
No certified or retail visual parity credit

No retail crossfade, pop timing, occlusion, draw-order, or GPU differential is claimed

slices.subject: Bind strict Fallout creature hit reactions
scope: FNV creature animation construction resolves one deterministic same-rig recovery KF and binds it to the CharacterController semantic group hit1
the resolver normalizes slash direction, case, and trailing separators
selection is fail-closed to six audited rig directories: nvsecuritron, dog, nvbighorner, nvmantis, radscorpion, and nvgecko
candidate priority is deterministic across authored hitreaction torso, hitreaction head, SpecialIdle hit torso, SpecialIdle hit head, MT SpecialIdle hit head, recoil, and hand-to-hand recoil names
the selected source must exist and pass the existing exact controller-target binding checks before it becomes the winning hit1 source
unknown and unaudited creature rigs reject even when they expose identically named recovery files
the raven rig rejects rather than misusing its unrelated fly-away idle or another rig's animation
the frozen Goodsprings census improves selectable creature hit recovery from zero to 16 of 37 actor placements and six of eight rig families
the exact Goodsprings placement numerator comprises Victor, Cheyenne, six bighorners, two mantises, one radscorpion, and five geckos
humanoid construction remains untouched because its source file contains protected pre-existing user changes
limitations: The selectable-source numerator is bounded to the frozen Goodsprings roster and is not a whole-corpus creature placement census
Seventeen Goodsprings humanoid placements remain unsupported because ESM4NpcAnimation was deliberately not changed
Four Goodsprings raven placements remain unsupported because the frozen official assets expose no audited hit or recoil KF for that rig
CharacterController still clears non-NPC creature recovery during an active upper-body attack before animation selection
Resolver tests use exact path fixtures and do not instantiate a retail KF, scene graph, controller map, or target actor
No natural shot-to-damage-to-visible-reaction journey or retail timing and pose differential was run
Hit direction, body-part selection, reaction variation, knockdown, death transitions, VATS, blood, impact effects, sound, and camera feedback remain outside this slice
No promoted-binary provenance
No certified parity credit

slices.subject: Preserve Fallout creature hit recovery
scope: CharacterController no longer discards hit recovery solely because a supported Fallout creature has an active upper-body action
the exception applies only while the existing upper-body state is above WeaponEquipped
the live Ptr must be an ESM4 Creature with a non-null base carrying the existing Fallout New Vegas identity flag
the live Animation must report an actually bound hit1 semantic group
a pure eight-case decision matrix proves NPC, game identity, and binding combinations
legacy and non-Fallout creatures retain the previous recovery-clearing behavior even if they expose a hit1 group
Fallout creatures without a bound hit1 group fail closed and retain the previous recovery-clearing behavior
NPC behavior remains unchanged
the separate strict source-binding slice remains the bounded provider for 16 of 37 Goodsprings placements across six of eight rig families
limitations: This slice preserves the recovery request but does not raise hit animation priority above an already-playing action
It adds no hit-reaction source, animation variant, directional or body-part selection, knockdown, death, blood, impact, sound, VATS, or camera behavior
Humanoid and raven Goodsprings placements remain unsupported by the separate source-binding contract
The decision test is pure and does not instantiate a CharacterController, retail actor, active attack, or animation scene graph
No natural active-attack shot-to-damage-to-visible-reaction journey or retail timing and pose differential was run
No promoted-binary provenance
No certified parity credit
